entities.description: The complete 46-stanza Cambridge version directly transcribed first by Varnhagen. The work occupies ff. 476v-479v within a large trilingual compendium; both boundary leaves also carry adjacent texts.
entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.1.1

physicalDescription.extent: ii + 633 + ii leaves; The Proverbs of Hendyng occupies ff. 476v-479v
notes: CUDL records several scribes and a broad English origin; it preserves competing West Midlands and possible Irish provenance hypotheses based on dialect. Those witness claims do not locate composition of the abstract poem.

entities.description: The complete 47-stanza Oxford version directly transcribed second by Varnhagen. It occupies ff. 140va-143ra; on f. 143r the poem ends in column a while the Old French Les proverbes del vilain begins in column b.
entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Digby 86

notes: Digital Bodleian describes a trilingual miscellany of religious, medical, and secular prose and verse made in Worcestershire. The witness date and origin do not automatically date or locate composition of the poem.

rights.basis: The poem and its manuscript witnesses are medieval and far predate U.S. copyright terms.
rights.component: Underlying anonymous medieval Proverbs of Hendyng text and witness readings
notes: This conclusion does not determine rights in modern photographs, interfaces, editions, encodings, or scholarship.

entities.description: The collateral complete version printed by Wright and Halliwell and used indirectly by Varnhagen for comparison. DIMEV separates this version as record 3383 because it prefixes a six-line opening beginning 'Mon that wol of wysdam heren' before the Jesus-Christ stanza.

physicalDescription.extent: 1* + 142 folios plus unfoliated paper flyleaves; the Hendyng version occupies ff. 125ra-127ra
notes: The relevant second part was written by the Harley scribe and is associated by the British Library with the West Midlands and Ludlow. This does not prove the abstract work's composition place.

entities.description: A collateral fragmentary witness represented by seventeen pieces once used as binding pads and now bound at the back of MS F.8. DIMEV describes their hypothetical continued foliation as 137-138 and identifies portions corresponding to five stanzas of the Digby text.

rights.basis: The versioned release includes a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 license.

entities.description: The version 1.0 Digital Index of Middle English Verse records the encoded incipit family as record 2800 / IMEV-NIMEV 1669 and separately records the prefixed Harley version as record 3383 / IMEV-NIMEV 2078.
entities.label: DIMEV records 2800 and 3383
notes: Record 2800 lists Digby 86, Gg.1.1, and the St John's F.8 fragments as three witnesses and describes aabccb six-line stanzas.

Record 3383 lists Harley MS 2253 and two short related fragments; its descriptive cross-reference points to unrelated record 2834 even though record 2800 points correctly to 3383. The inconsistency is preserved as a data question.
The record split is catalog evidence about version identity, not proof that the encoded Gg and Digby streams are separate authored works.

rights.basis: The versioned release includes a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 license.
rights.component: DIMEV version 1.0 data release

next_steps: Consult the most recent critical edition and textual study, including the St John's fragments.
Retain DIMEV records 2800 and 3383 as distinct catalog identities while testing their stanza-level relationships.
Do not turn shared proverbial material or Varnhagen's concordance into unsupported direct-copying edges among surviving manuscripts.
openQuestions.question: How should the complete Gg.1.1, Digby 86, and prefixed Harley 2253 versions and the smaller fragments be related in a current critical model of The Proverbs of Hendyng?

next_steps: Review current work on the Hendyng-Marcolf framing and authorship of the compilation.
Keep anonymous creation separate from the text's recurring Quod Hending attribution until historical identity is demonstrated.
openQuestions.question: Does Hendyng represent a historical author, a legendary wise persona, or a name constructed from Middle English hende to authorize traditional material?

next_steps: Ask St John's Special Collections for its current F.8 catalog record and a complete host-volume image inventory.
Request both sides of all seventeen fragments and verify DIMEV's hypothetical ff. 137-138 reconstruction.
Record item-specific reuse terms before retaining or publishing supplied images.
openQuestions.question: What are the current mounting, date, material sequence, exact recto-verso order, public image status, and complete host-object extent of the Hendyng fragments in St John's MS F.8 (145)?

next_steps: Recheck the exact catalog and Digitised Manuscripts route after service restoration.
If public systematic retrieval becomes authorized, cache the complete codex and map ff. 125ra-127ra separately.
Until then use Wright-Halliwell as Varnhagen's evidenced comparison intermediary and do not imply direct image inspection.
openQuestions.question: When will the British Library's claimed full digital coverage of Harley MS 2253 again expose an auditable image inventory, and what terms will govern download and reuse?

next_steps: Report the asymmetric cross-reference to DIMEV with exact release locators.
Preserve the verified 2800-to-3383 link and do not propagate 2834 as a Hendyng relation without correction.
openQuestions.question: Is DIMEV record 3383's reference to unrelated record 2834 a data error intended to point back to record 2800?

next_steps: Review authorial-language and rhyme evidence across all complete versions.
Keep manuscript production, scribal dialect, possible provenance, and abstract composition place distinct.
openQuestions.question: Where within medieval English-language culture was the work composed, as distinct from Digby 86's Worcestershire production and the competing West Midlands or Irish provenance hypotheses for Gg.1.1?

next_steps: Ask U-M for item-level production records and recheck the exact interface after the automated challenge is lifted.
Do not infer OCR, manual keying, or a release date from the 2016 repository review note alone.
openQuestions.question: What exact U-M keying, transformation, and publication history produced the pinned CME00091 XML artifact?

relations.subjectLabel: Cameron Louis, Authority in Middle English Proverb Literature (1998)

reviewNotes: The two XML divisions are parallel complete witness versions of one intellectual work, not two separate works and not ninety-three independent works.
DIMEV record 2800 and the related prefixed Harley record 3383 remain distinct catalog identities; their relationship is recorded without silently merging them.
Both complete manuscript caches retain their whole physical codices. The work starts and ends on shared codex surfaces, and the exact folio and canvas ranges are mapped separately.
Varnhagen's Harley comparison is routed through Wright and Halliwell's printed text because the article evidences H(alliwell) readings, not physical consultation of Harley MS 2253.
Provider image licenses and current access conditions remain separate from the public-domain status of the medieval text and old print editions.

lineage.summary: CME00091 is the checked-in copy of a University of Michigan encoding of printed pages 182-200 from Hermann Varnhagen's 1881 Anglia article on The Proverbs of Hendyng. The XML omits Varnhagen's introduction and three-witness stanza-concordance table on pages 180-181, then serializes two complete manuscript versions of one work: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.1.1, ff. 476v-479v, and Bodleian Library MS Digby 86, ff. 140va-143ra. Varnhagen directly transcribed those codices and compared them with the Harley MS 2253 version through Wright and Halliwell's 1845 printing; his notes identify the comparison source as H(alliwell), so no direct Varnhagen-to-Harley consultation is claimed. DIMEV v1.0 records the encoded incipit family as record 2800 and treats the Harley version with its six-line prefixed opening separately as record 3383. Complete public facsimiles of Gg.1.1 and Digby 86, complete scans of Varnhagen and Wright-Halliwell, DIMEV v1.0, and a modern scholarly article are cached locally. Manuscript-image licenses are noncommercial even though the underlying medieval work and nineteenth-century editions are public domain in the United States.

catalogSummary.author: Anonymous

catalogSummary.title: The Proverbs of Hendyng

catalogingSubject.label: The Proverbs of Hendyng: Gg.1.1 and Digby 86 versions
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The cataloged subject is Hermann Varnhagen's 1881 parallel publication of two complete manuscript versions of The Proverbs of Hendyng, from Cambridge University Library MS Gg.1.1 and Bodleian Library MS Digby 86, as represented in CME00091. The two encoded DIV1 elements and their 93 stanza groups are two transmission versions of one work, not 93 independent works. Varnhagen's German introduction, comparison table, and apparatus, the U-M encoding, the repository XML, and the physical witnesses remain distinct descriptive and lineage layers.

contentParts.label: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.1.1 version
notes: The first folio also contains preceding French text, and the final folio continues with a new Latin item; those neighboring texts are not part of Hendyng.
The 304 XML L elements comprise 276 stanza-body lines and 28 lines inside multi-line epigraphs.
contentParts.summary: Varnhagen's complete transcription of the 46-stanza Gg.1.1 version, beginning with an Old French rubric and transmitted at fols. 476v–479v.

contentParts.label: Bodleian Library MS Digby 86 version
notes: On fol. 143r the poem ends in column a and Les proverbes del vilain begins in column b; the shared surface does not make the Old French work part of Hendyng.
The 304 XML L elements comprise 282 stanza-body lines and 22 lines inside multi-line epigraphs.
contentParts.summary: Varnhagen's complete transcription of the 47-stanza Digby 86 version, under the rubric Hending þe hende and transmitted at fols. 140v–143r.

assertion.notes: This is a broad scholarly date for the work, not a claim that either surviving version preserves an authorial original.

scope.description: First known full modern printing of the Harley 2253 version, which is related to but not one of CME00091's two complete version streams

extent.description: Encoding metrics for the two version streams: 46 plus 47 stanza groups and 608 XML L elements. Of those L elements, 558 are the six-line stanza bodies and 50 are multi-line proverbial epigraphs; 70 additional single-line proverbial sayings are encoded as P rather than L. These are representation counts across two versions, not an intrinsic count of 93 separate works.

formStatements.description: Six-line aabccb stanza bodies, normally followed by a proverbial saying and the attribution formula Quod Hending; brief rubrics and formulae do not make the work mixed prose and verse.

scope.description: Recurring Quod Hending attribution formulae within otherwise Middle English stanzas

notes: The XML's 93 stanza groups are a serialization of two complete witness versions (46 plus 47), not a canonical 93-stanza work or a collection of 93 independent works.

Varnhagen directly prints Gg.1.1 and Digby 86 but identifies the Harley comparison readings as H(alliwell); the lineage therefore routes those comparisons through the 1845 Wright–Halliwell edition rather than claiming direct consultation of Harley 2253.
Modern German in Varnhagen's introduction and notes belongs to the scholarly edition layer, not the medieval work-language statement.

The complete CUL and Bodleian facsimiles are locally cached outside Git with exact IIIF links and work-canvas ranges in the lineage manifest; Bodleian's separate 23-image selection has no overlap with fols. 140v–143r.

openQuestions.question: What are the present mounting, date, exact textual contents, and full photographic coverage of the Hendyng fragments in St John's College Cambridge MS F.8 (145)?

next_steps: Consult a current linguistic study of all three major versions rather than transferring any one witness's provenance to the work.
openQuestions.question: Can the place or dialect region of the work's composition be distinguished from the production places and linguistic profiles of its surviving witnesses?

assertion.notes: The work belongs to the Middle English literary tradition; its exact place of composition is unknown.

scope.description: One catalog hypothesis concerning production or textual affiliations of Gg.1.1

assertion.notes: This witness-level hypothesis must not be promoted to the work's place of composition.
Modern scholarship does not securely identify a historical author.
Hendyng functions as the recurring proverbial authority; the name may be a literary construction from hende rather than a recoverable historical author.

summaries.value: The Proverbs of Hendyng is a thirteenth-century Middle English wisdom poem built from end-rhymed stanzas of counsel. A stanza develops advice about religious, domestic, economic, or social conduct and culminates in a compact proverbial saying attributed to Hendyng. The collection addresses practical Christian ethics for an adult audience that modern scholarship understands as potentially both monastic and lay. CME00091 presents Varnhagen's complete transcriptions of the Gg.1.1 and Digby 86 versions in sequence.

The first encoded division gives 46 stanzas from Cambridge University Library MS Gg.1.1; the second gives 47 stanzas from Bodleian Library MS Digby 86. Varnhagen's comparison reports 30 stanzas common to all three major versions, further partial overlap, and seven stanzas unique to each version. The two streams therefore transmit overlapping but materially different forms of one proverb work.

The record catalogs the two complete version streams as parts of an edited selection, not as independent works and not as 93 works. The prefixed Harley 2253 form indexed separately as DIMEV record 3383 / IMEV 2078 is represented as a related version. The tiny St John's F.8 fragments are supporting witnesses whose exact relationship remains incompletely described.

notes: The complete article title is Zu mittelenglischen Gedichten. XI. Zu den Sprichwörtern Hending's.

scope.description: Varnhagen's German heading for the edited texts
Normalized representative incipit for the Gg.1.1 and Digby 86 tradition; wording and spelling vary by witness
